texto: The silence of a convent at night is the silence of the grave. Too far removed from the busy world without for external sounds to penetrate the thick walls, whilst within no slamming door, nor wandering foot, nor sacrilegious voice breaks in upon the stillness, the slightest noise strikes upon the ear with a fearful distinctness. ("The Monk's Story")
explicacao: Citação de Catherine Crowe sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. “The silence of a convent at night is the silence of the grave. frase_original: The silence of a convent at night is the silence of the grave. Too far removed from the busy world without for external sounds to penetrate the thick walls, whilst within no slamming door, nor wandering foot, nor sacrilegious voice breaks in upon the stillness, the slightest noise strikes upon the ear with a fearful distinctness. ("The Monk's Story")

texto: The ignorant frighten children with ghosts, and the better educated assure them there is no such thing. Our understanding may believe the latter, but our instincts believe the former; so that, out of this education, we retain the terror, and just believe enough to make it very troublesome whenever we are placed in circumstances that awaken it.
explicacao: Citação de Catherine Crowe sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “The ignorant frighten children with ghosts, and the better educated assure them there is no such …” articula educacao, fear, ghosts em registro de educacao, com relevância para contextos como ghosts e superstition.
